candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Scholars Program | Department of Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: spend four weeks on an internal medicine elective at the University of Washington Medical Center or Harborview Medical Center
Students will rotate at one or a combination of our teaching hospitals/clinics: UW Medical Center, Harborview Medical Center, and Roosevelt Clinic.

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: UW Anesthesiology does NOT accept international visiting students (per search summary, would need separate verification). UW OBGYN limits international electives due to limited numbers. Internal Medicine VSP is US-only verbatim.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: No central UW Medicine visiting-students hub page found that documents all departments uniformly. Each department's visiting-students lane has its own eligibility and the central admin policy was not directly captured verbatim on a single hub.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) DEPARTMENT_LEVEL_SOURCE — different UW departments have different eligibility; the central UW Medicine hub page needs follow-up retrieval; (b) Internal Medicine VSP at Harborview is US-only with funding; (c) Dermatology lists Harborview as placement but doesn't specify audience; (d) Anesthesiology + OB/GYN per search summary do not accept international (needs verification). Manual retry queued for: central UW SOM visiting-students hub.
plainEnglishSummary: Harborview Medical Center is a major UW Medicine teaching site for visiting students. Internal Medicine's Visiting Scholars Program at Harborview is US-medical-school-only with funded travel/lodging. Dermatology lists Harborview as a placement site. UW has different rules per department: some accept international (broad hub mentions 'approximately 50 visiting students, US and international per year'), some specifically do not (Anesthesiology, OB/GYN). The unified UW visiting-students central hub policy was not captured verbatim this turn.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Washington gained its first P101 packet — Harborview placement is documented for IM (US-only) and Dermatology (audience unverified); WA was a P0 state gap before this packet.
